title: Portable suction-device outage escalation plan
summary: A clinician-approved continuity plan for a prescribed portable suction device, backup power, charged batteries, consumables, cleaning, early relocation, and emergency respiratory warning signs.
content:
A clinician-approved continuity plan for a prescribed portable suction device, backup power, charged batteries, consumables, cleaning, early relocation, and emergency respiratory warning signs.

Use the materials as a compact field kit. Work through the steps in order, pause whenever a warning or stop condition applies, and write down the outcome so another person can continue safely.
materials: charged approved batteries and power supply
steps: Ask the clinical team to document normal use, backup method, battery duration, cleaning, consumable quantity, transport, and symptoms that require 112.
Keep charged approved power sources and unopened consumables together, label compatibility, and test the complete backup setup on the recommended schedule.
At an outage record the start time and remaining capacity, contact the supplier or clinical team early, and relocate before the backup becomes critically low.
Call 112 for severe breathing difficulty, blue or grey colour, inability to clear secretions with the prescribed plan, reduced consciousness, or device and backup failure.
warnings: Do not change pressure, tubing, technique, or cleaning method without the prescribed instructions.
Do not reuse single-use contaminated supplies merely to extend stock.
common_mistakes: Starting Portable suction-device outage escalation plan without first checking immediate danger and official instructions.
Failing to record time, location, responsible person, and unresolved risks for Portable suction-device outage escalation plan.
alternatives: Use a paper-only version of Portable suction-device outage escalation plan when power or connectivity is unavailable.
Assign one trained helper to simplify Portable suction-device outage escalation plan when the group is stressed or short-handed.
failure_signs: Conditions become more dangerous while carrying out Portable suction-device outage escalation plan.
Critical information cannot be confirmed or the assigned helper cannot continue Portable suction-device outage escalation plan safely.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue Portable suction-device outage escalation plan when it requires entering an unstable, contaminated, flooded, burning, electrically hazardous, or officially closed area.
Do not improvise medical, electrical, structural, or rescue actions beyond your training while using Portable suction-device outage escalation plan.
short_term: ['Within the first operational period, Portable suction-device outage escalation plan should produce a dated status, one responsible person, and a safe next action.', 'Repeat the essential check for Portable suction-device outage escalation plan whenever conditions, official advice, or available resources change.']
long_term: ['Include lessons from Portable suction-device outage escalation plan in the household or community emergency plan and resupply list.', 'Review the records from Portable suction-device outage escalation plan after the event and correct gaps before the next emergency.']
if_method_fails: ['If Portable suction-device outage escalation plan cannot be completed, move to the safest verified location, notify the responsible contact, and record what remains unresolved.', 'Use official emergency services or qualified specialists when the limits of Portable suction-device outage escalation plan are reached.']
environment_notes: ['Adapt Portable suction-device outage escalation plan to local terrain, weather, building type, transport access, language, disability needs, and official restrictions.', 'In Greece, use 112 for urgent emergencies and follow Civil Protection or local-authority instructions relevant to Portable suction-device outage escalation plan.']
